title: Java Stream Collect
description: Practice stream collection with Collectors.toList and toList.

completionChecks.prompt: What does emails contain after this stream pipeline runs?
choices: Compilation fails because toList() is not a terminal operation
completionChecks.explanation: The second pipeline maps every user to email() and then materializes those mapped values with Stream.toList().

completionChecks.prompt: What does activeNames contain after this code runs?
choices: [] because collect(Collectors.toList()) is not a terminal operation
completionChecks.explanation: Only Ada is active, so the first pipeline filters to Ada and maps to the name, producing [Ada]; collect(Collectors.toList()) is a terminal operation.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens after emails is created with stream().map(User::email).toList() and code calls emails.add("new@example.com")?
choices: The address is appended because toList always returns an ArrayList
Compilation fails because add is not declared on List
The call is ignored and emails stays unchanged
completionChecks.explanation: Stream.toList returns an unmodifiable result, a common trap when comparing it with Collectors.toList().
